NKJV.1: Then the scribes and Pharisees who were from Jerusalem came to Jesus, saying,
NKJV.2: “Why do Your disciples transgress the tradition of the elders? For they do not wash their hands when they eat bread.”
NKJV.3: He answered and said to them, “Why do you also transgress the commandment of God because of your tradition?
NKJV.4: For God commanded, saying,  ‘Honor your father and your mother’;  and,  ‘He who curses father or mother, let him be put to death.’
NKJV.5: But you say, ‘Whoever says to his father or mother, “Whatever profit you might have received from me is a gift to God”—
NKJV.6: then he need not honor his father or mother.’ Thus you have made the commandment of God of no effect by your tradition.
NKJV.7: Hypocrites! Well did Isaiah prophesy about you, saying:
NKJV.8: ‘These people draw near to Me with their mouth, And honor Me with their lips, But their heart is far from Me.
NKJV.9: And in vain they worship Me, Teaching as doctrines the commandments of men.’ ”
NKJV.10: When He had called the multitude to Himself, He said to them, “Hear and understand:
NKJV.11: Not what goes into the mouth defiles a man; but what comes out of the mouth, this defiles a man.”
NKJV.12: Then His disciples came and said to Him, “Do You know that the Pharisees were offended when they heard this saying?”
NKJV.13: But He answered and said, “Every plant which My heavenly Father has not planted will be uprooted.
NKJV.14: Let them alone.  They are blind leaders of the blind. And if the blind leads the blind, both will fall into a ditch.”
NKJV.15: Then Peter answered and said to Him, “Explain this parable to us.”
NKJV.16: So Jesus said, “Are you also still without understanding?
NKJV.17: Do you not yet understand that  whatever enters the mouth goes into the stomach and is eliminated?
NKJV.18: But  those things which proceed out of the mouth come from the heart, and they defile a man.
NKJV.19: For out of the heart proceed evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, fornications, thefts, false witness, blasphemies.
NKJV.20: These are the things which defile a man, but to eat with unwashed hands does not defile a man.”
NKJV.21: Then Jesus went out from there and departed to the region of Tyre and Sidon.
NKJV.22: And behold, a woman of Canaan came from that region and cried out to Him, saying, “Have mercy on me, O Lord, Son of David! My daughter is severely demon-possessed.”
NKJV.23: But He answered her not a word. And His disciples came and urged Him, saying, “Send her away, for she cries out after us.”
NKJV.24: But He answered and said, “I was not sent except to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”
NKJV.25: Then she came and worshiped Him, saying, “Lord, help me!”
NKJV.26: But He answered and said, “It is not good to take the children’s bread and throw it to the little dogs.”
NKJV.27: And she said, “Yes, Lord, yet even the little dogs eat the crumbs which fall from their masters’ table.”
NKJV.28: Then Jesus answered and said to her, “O woman, great is your faith! Let it be to you as you desire.” And her daughter was healed from that very hour.
NKJV.29: Jesus departed from there, skirted the Sea of Galilee, and went up on the mountain and sat down there.
NKJV.30: Then great multitudes came to Him, having with them the lame, blind, mute, maimed, and many others; and they laid them down at Jesus’ feet, and He healed them.
NKJV.31: So the multitude marveled when they saw the mute speaking, the maimed made whole, the lame walking, and the blind seeing; and they glorified the God of Israel.
NKJV.32: Now Jesus called His disciples to Himself and said, “I have compassion on the multitude, because they have now continued with Me three days and have nothing to eat. And I do not want to send them away hungry, lest they faint on the way.”
NKJV.33: Then His disciples said to Him, “Where could we get enough bread in the wilderness to fill such a great multitude?”
NKJV.34: Jesus said to them, “How many loaves do you have?” And they said, “Seven, and a few little fish.”
NKJV.35: So He commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground.
NKJV.36: And He took the seven loaves and the fish and gave thanks, broke them and gave them to His disciples; and the disciples gave to the multitude.
NKJV.37: So they all ate and were filled, and they took up seven large baskets full of the fragments that were left.
NKJV.38: Now those who ate were four thousand men, besides women and children.
NKJV.39: And He sent away the multitude, got into the boat, and came to the region of Magdala.

ESV.1: Then Pharisees and scribes came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said,
ESV.2: “Why do your disciples break the tradition of the elders? For they do not wash their hands when they eat.”
ESV.3: He answered them, “And why do you break the commandment of God for the sake of your tradition?
ESV.4: For God commanded, ‘Honor your father and your mother,’ and, ‘Whoever reviles father or mother must surely die.’
ESV.5: But you say, ‘If anyone tells his father or his mother, “What you would have gained from me is given to God,”
ESV.6: he need not honor his father.’ So for the sake of your tradition you have made void the word  of God.
ESV.7: You hypocrites! Well did Isaiah prophesy of you, when he said:
ESV.8: “‘This people honors me with their lips, but their heart is far from me;
ESV.9: in vain do they worship me, teaching as doctrines the commandments of men.’”
ESV.10: And he called the people to him and said to them,  “Hear and understand:
ESV.11: it is not what goes into the mouth that defiles a person, but what comes out of the mouth; this defiles a person.”
ESV.12: Then the disciples came and said to him, “Do you know that the Pharisees were offended when they heard this saying?”
ESV.13: He answered,  “Every plant that my heavenly Father has not planted will be rooted up.
ESV.14: Let them alone; they are blind guides.  And if the blind lead the blind, both will fall into a pit.”
ESV.15: But Peter said to him, “Explain the parable to us.”
ESV.16: And he said,  “Are you also still without understanding?
ESV.17: Do you not see that whatever goes into the mouth passes into the stomach and is expelled?
ESV.18: But what comes out of the mouth proceeds from the heart, and this defiles a person.
ESV.19: For out of the heart come evil thoughts, murder, adultery, sexual immorality, theft, false witness, slander.
ESV.20: These are what defile a person. But to eat with unwashed hands does not defile anyone.”
ESV.21: And Jesus went away from there and withdrew to the district of Tyre and Sidon.
ESV.22: And behold, a Canaanite woman from that region came out and was crying, “Have mercy on me, O Lord, Son of David; my daughter is severely oppressed by a demon.”
ESV.23: But he did not answer her a word. And his disciples came and begged him, saying, “Send her away, for she is crying out after us.”
ESV.24: He answered,  “I was sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”
ESV.25: But she came and knelt before him, saying, “Lord, help me.”
ESV.26: And he answered, “It is not right to take the children's bread and throw it to the dogs.”
ESV.27: She said, “Yes, Lord, yet even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their masters' table.”
ESV.28: Then Jesus answered her, “O woman, great is your faith! Be it done for you as you desire.” And her daughter was healed instantly.
ESV.29: Jesus went on from there and walked beside the Sea of Galilee. And he went up on the mountain and sat down there.
ESV.30: And great crowds came to him, bringing with them the lame, the blind, the crippled, the mute, and many others, and they put them at his feet, and he healed them,
ESV.31: so that the crowd wondered, when they saw the mute speaking, the crippled healthy, the lame walking, and the blind seeing. And they glorified the God of Israel.
ESV.32: Then Jesus called his disciples to him and said,  “I have compassion on the crowd because they have been with me now three days and have nothing to eat. And I am unwilling to send them away hungry, lest they faint on the way.”
ESV.33: And the disciples said to him, “Where are we to get enough bread in such a desolate place to feed so great a crowd?”
ESV.34: And Jesus said to them, “How many loaves do you have?” They said, “Seven, and a few small fish.”
ESV.35: And directing the crowd to sit down on the ground,
ESV.36: he took the seven loaves and the fish, and having given thanks he broke them and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
ESV.37: And they all ate and were satisfied. And they took up seven baskets full of the broken pieces left over.
ESV.38: Those who ate were four thousand men, besides women and children.
ESV.39: And after sending away the crowds, he got into the boat and went to the region of Magadan.

NASB.1: Then some Pharisees and scribes came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said,
NASB.2: 'Why do Your disciples break the tradition of the elders? For they do not wash their hands when they eat bread.'
NASB.3: And He answered and said to them, 'Why do you yourselves transgress the commandment of God for the sake of your tradition?
NASB.4: 'For God said, 'HONOR YOUR FATHER AND MOTHER,' and, 'HE WHO SPEAKS EVIL OF FATHER OR MOTHER IS TO BE PUT TO DEATH.'
NASB.5: 'But you say, 'Whoever says to [his] father or mother, 'Whatever I have that would help you has been given [to God],'
NASB.6: he is not to honor his father or his mother.' And [by this] you invalidated the word of God for the sake of your tradition.
NASB.7: 'You hypocrites, rightly did Isaiah prophesy of you:
NASB.8: 'THIS PEOPLE HONORS ME WITH THEIR LIPS, BUT THEIR HEART IS FAR AWAY FROM ME.
NASB.9: 'BUT IN VAIN DO THEY WORSHIP ME, TEACHING AS DOCTRINES THE PRECEPTS OF MEN.''
NASB.10: After Jesus called the crowd to Him, He said to them, 'Hear and understand.
NASB.11: '[It is] not what enters into the mouth [that] defiles the man, but what proceeds out of the mouth, this defiles the man.'
NASB.12: Then the disciples came and said to Him, 'Do You know that the Pharisees were offended when they heard this statement?'
NASB.13: But He answered and said, 'Every plant which My heavenly Father did not plant shall be uprooted.
NASB.14: 'Let them alone; they are blind guides of the blind. And if a blind man guides a blind man, both will fall into a pit.'
NASB.15: Peter said to Him, 'Explain the parable to us.'
NASB.16: Jesus said, 'Are you still lacking in understanding also?
NASB.17: 'Do you not understand that everything that goes into the mouth passes into the stomach, and is eliminated?
NASB.18: 'But the things that proceed out of the mouth come from the heart, and those defile the man.
NASB.19: 'For out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, fornications, thefts, false witness, slanders.
NASB.20: 'These are the things which defile the man; but to eat with unwashed hands does not defile the man.'
NASB.21: Jesus went away from there, and withdrew into the district of Tyre and Sidon.
NASB.22: And a Canaanite woman from that region came out and [began] to cry out, saying, 'Have mercy on me, Lord, Son of David; my daughter is cruelly demon-possessed.'
NASB.23: But He did not answer her a word. And His disciples came and implored Him, saying, 'Send her away, because she keeps shouting at us.'
NASB.24: But He answered and said, 'I was sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.'
NASB.25: But she came and [began] to bow down before Him, saying, 'Lord, help me!'
NASB.26: And He answered and said, 'It is not good to take the children's bread and throw it to the dogs.'
NASB.27: But she said, 'Yes, Lord; but even the dogs feed on the crumbs which fall from their masters' table.'
NASB.28: Then Jesus said to her, 'O woman, your faith is great; it shall be done for you as you wish.' And her daughter was healed at once.
NASB.29: Departing from there, Jesus went along by the Sea of Galilee, and having gone up on the mountain, He was sitting there.
NASB.30: And large crowds came to Him, bringing with them [those who were] lame, crippled, blind, mute, and many others, and they laid them down at His feet; and He healed them.
NASB.31: So the crowd marveled as they saw the mute speaking, the crippled restored, and the lame walking, and the blind seeing; and they glorified the God of Israel.
NASB.32: And Jesus called His disciples to Him, and said, 'I feel compassion for the people, because they have remained with Me now three days and have nothing to eat; and I do not want to send them away hungry, for they might faint on the way.'
NASB.33: The disciples said to Him, 'Where would we get so many loaves in [this] desolate place to satisfy such a large crowd?'
NASB.34: And Jesus said to them, 'How many loaves do you have?' And they said, 'Seven, and a few small fish.'
NASB.35: And He directed the people to sit down on the ground;
NASB.36: and He took the seven loaves and the fish; and giving thanks, He broke them and started giving them to the disciples, and the disciples [gave them] to the people.
NASB.37: And they all ate and were satisfied, and they picked up what was left over of the broken pieces, seven large baskets full.
NASB.38: And those who ate were four thousand men, besides women and children.
NASB.39: And sending away the crowds, Jesus got into the boat and came to the region of Magadan.

NLT.1: Some Pharisees and teachers of religious law now arrived from Jerusalem to see Jesus. They asked him,
NLT.2: “Why do your disciples disobey our age-old tradition? For they ignore our tradition of ceremonial hand washing before they eat.”
NLT.3: Jesus replied, “And why do you, by your traditions, violate the direct commandments of God?
NLT.4: For instance, God says, ‘Honor your father and mother,’ and ‘Anyone who speaks disrespectfully of father or mother must be put to death.’
NLT.5: But you say it is all right for people to say to their parents, ‘Sorry, I can’t help you. For I have vowed to give to God what I would have given to you.’
NLT.6: In this way, you say they don’t need to honor their parents. And so you cancel the word of God for the sake of your own tradition.
NLT.7: You hypocrites! Isaiah was right when he prophesied about you, for he wrote,
NLT.8:
‘These people honor me with their lips,
but their hearts are far from me.
NLT.9:
Their worship is a farce,
for they teach man-made ideas as commands from God.’ ”
NLT.10: Then Jesus called to the crowd to come and hear. “Listen,” he said, “and try to understand.
NLT.11: It’s not what goes into your mouth that defiles you; you are defiled by the words that come out of your mouth.”
NLT.12: Then the disciples came to him and asked, “Do you realize you offended the Pharisees by what you just said?”
NLT.13: Jesus replied, “Every plant not planted by my heavenly Father will be uprooted,
NLT.14: so ignore them. They are blind guides leading the blind, and if one blind person guides another, they will both fall into a ditch.”
NLT.15: Then Peter said to Jesus, “Explain to us the parable that says people aren’t defiled by what they eat.”
NLT.17: “Anything you eat passes through the stomach and then goes into the sewer.
NLT.18: But the words you speak come from the heart — that’s what defiles you.
NLT.19: For from the heart come evil thoughts, murder, adultery, all sexual immorality, theft, lying, and slander.
NLT.20: These are what defile you. Eating with unwashed hands will never defile you.”
NLT.21: Then Jesus left Galilee and went north to the region of Tyre and Sidon.
NLT.22: A Gentile woman who lived there came to him, pleading, “Have mercy on me, O Lord, Son of David! For my daughter is possessed by a demon that torments her severely.”
NLT.23: But Jesus gave her no reply, not even a word. Then his disciples urged him to send her away. “Tell her to go away,” they said. “She is bothering us with all her begging.”
NLT.24: Then Jesus said to the woman, “I was sent only to help God’s lost sheep — the people of Israel.”
NLT.25: But she came and worshiped him, pleading again, “Lord, help me!”
NLT.26: Jesus responded, “It isn’t right to take food from the children and throw it to the dogs.”
NLT.27: She replied, “That’s true, Lord, but even dogs are allowed to eat the scraps that fall beneath their masters’ table.”
NLT.28: “Dear woman,” Jesus said to her, “your faith is great. Your request is granted.” And her daughter was instantly healed.
NLT.29: Jesus returned to the Sea of Galilee and climbed a hill and sat down.
NLT.30: A vast crowd brought to him people who were lame, blind, crippled, those who couldn’t speak, and many others. They laid them before Jesus, and he healed them all.
NLT.31: The crowd was amazed! Those who hadn’t been able to speak were talking, the crippled were made well, the lame were walking, and the blind could see again! And they praised the God of Israel.
NLT.32: Then Jesus called his disciples and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been here with me for three days, and they have nothing left to eat. I don’t want to send them away hungry, or they will faint along the way.”
NLT.33: The disciples replied, “Where would we get enough food here in the wilderness for such a huge crowd?”
NLT.34: Jesus asked, “How much bread do you have?” They replied, “Seven loaves, and a few small fish.”
NLT.35: So Jesus told all the people to sit down on the ground.
NLT.36: Then he took the seven loaves and the fish, thanked God for them, and broke them into pieces. He gave them to the disciples, who distributed the food to the crowd.
NLT.37: They all ate as much as they wanted. Afterward, the disciples picked up seven large baskets of leftover food.
NLT.38: There were 4,000 men who were fed that day, in addition to all the women and children.
NLT.39: Then Jesus sent the people home, and he got into a boat and crossed over to the region of Magadan.

WEB.1: Then Pharisees and scribes came to Jesus from Jerusalem, saying,
WEB.2: “Why do your disciples disobey the tradition of the elders? For they don’t wash their hands when they eat bread.”
WEB.3: He answered them, “Why do you also disobey the commandment of God because of your tradition?
WEB.4: For God commanded, ‘Honor your father and your mother,’  and, ‘He who speaks evil of father or mother, let him be put to death.’
WEB.5: But you say, ‘Whoever may tell his father or his mother, “Whatever help you might otherwise have gotten from me is a gift devoted to God,”
WEB.6: he shall not honor his father or mother.’ You have made the commandment of God void because of your tradition.
WEB.7: You hypocrites! Well did Isaiah prophesy of you, saying,
WEB.8: ‘These people draw near to me with their mouth,  and honor me with their lips;  but their heart is far from me.
WEB.9: And in vain do they worship me,  teaching as doctrine rules made by men.’”
WEB.10: He summoned the multitude, and said to them, “Hear, and understand.
WEB.11: That which enters into the mouth doesn’t defile the man; but that which proceeds out of the mouth, this defiles the man.”
WEB.12: Then the disciples came, and said to him, “Do you know that the Pharisees were offended, when they heard this saying?”
WEB.13: But he answered, “Every plant which my heavenly Father didn’t plant will be uprooted.
WEB.14: Leave them alone. They are blind guides of the blind. If the blind guide the blind, both will fall into a pit.”
WEB.15: Peter answered him, “Explain the parable to us.”
WEB.16: So Jesus said, “Do you also still not understand?
WEB.17: Don’t you understand that whatever goes into the mouth passes into the belly, and then out of the body?
WEB.18: But the things which proceed out of the mouth come out of the heart, and they defile the man.
WEB.19: For out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, sexual sins, thefts, false testimony, and blasphemies.
WEB.20: These are the things which defile the man; but to eat with unwashed hands doesn’t defile the man.”
WEB.21: Jesus went out from there, and withdrew into the region of Tyre and Sidon.
WEB.22: Behold, a Canaanite woman came out from those borders, and cried, saying, “Have mercy on me, Lord, you son of David! My daughter is severely possessed by a demon!”
WEB.23: But he answered her not a word. His disciples came and begged him, saying, “Send her away; for she cries after us.”
WEB.24: But he answered, “I wasn’t sent to anyone but the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”
WEB.25: But she came and worshiped him, saying, “Lord, help me.”
WEB.26: But he answered, “It is not appropriate to take the children’s bread and throw it to the dogs.”
WEB.27: But she said, “Yes, Lord, but even the dogs eat the crumbs which fall from their masters’ table.”
WEB.28: Then Jesus answered her, “Woman, great is your faith! Be it done to you even as you desire.” And her daughter was healed from that hour.
WEB.29: Jesus departed there, and came near to the sea of Galilee; and he went up into the mountain, and sat there.
WEB.30: Great multitudes came to him, having with them the lame, blind, mute, maimed, and many others, and they put them down at his feet. He healed them,
WEB.31: so that the multitude wondered when they saw the mute speaking, the injured healed, the lame walking, and the blind seeing — and they glorified the God of Israel.
WEB.32: Jesus summoned his disciples and said, “I have compassion on the multitude, because they continue with me now three days and have nothing to eat. I don’t want to send them away fasting, or they might faint on the way.”
WEB.33: The disciples said to him, “Where should we get so many loaves in a deserted place as to satisfy so great a multitude?”
WEB.34: Jesus said to them, “How many loaves do you have?” They said, “Seven, and a few small fish.”
WEB.35: He commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground;
WEB.36: and he took the seven loaves and the fish. He gave thanks and broke them, and gave to the disciples, and the disciples to the multitudes.
WEB.37: They all ate, and were filled. They took up seven baskets full of the broken pieces that were left over.
WEB.38: Those who ate were four thousand men, besides women and children.
WEB.39: Then he sent away the multitudes, got into the boat, and came into the borders of Magdala.

CSB17.1: Then <sup>ⓕ</sup> Jesus was approached by Pharisees and scribes from Jerusalem, who asked, <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.2: “Why do your disciples <sup>ⓗ</sup> break the tradition of the elders? For they don’t wash their hands when they eat.” <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.3: He answered them, “Why do you break God’s commandment because of your tradition?
CSB17.4: For God said: [2]Honor your father and your mother; [3] ⓙand, Whoever speaks evil of father or mother must be put to death. [4] ⓚ
CSB17.5: But you say, ‘Whoever tells his father or mother, “Whatever benefit you might have received from me is a gift committed to the temple,”
CSB17.6: he does not have to honor his father.’ <sup>[5]</sup>In this way, you have nullified the word of God <sup>[6]</sup>because of your tradition.
CSB17.7: Hypocrites! Isaiah prophesied correctly about you when he said:
CSB17.8:
This people [7] honors me with their lips,
but their heart is far from me.
CSB17.9:
They worship me in vain,
teaching as doctrines human commands.” [8] ⓛ
CSB17.10: Summoning the crowd, he told them, “Listen and understand:
CSB17.11: It’s not what goes into the mouth that defiles <sup>ⓜ</sup>a person, but what comes out of the mouth — this defiles a person.” <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.12: Then the disciples came up and told him, “Do you know that the Pharisees took offense when they heard what you said?”
CSB17.13: He replied, “Every plant that my heavenly Father didn’t plant will be uprooted. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.14: Leave them alone! They are blind guides. <sup>[9]</sup>And if the blind guide the blind, both will fall into a pit.” <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.15: Then Peter said, “Explain this parable to us.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.17: “Don’t you realize <sup>[11]</sup>that whatever goes into the mouth passes into the stomach and is eliminated? <sup>[12]</sup>
CSB17.18: But what comes out of the mouth comes from the heart, and this defiles a person. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.19: For from the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, sexual immoralities, thefts, false testimonies, <sup>ⓔ</sup>slander. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.20: These are the things that defile a person; but eating with unwashed hands does not defile a person.” <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.21: When Jesus left there, <sup>ⓗ</sup> he withdrew to the area of Tyre and Sidon. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.22: Just then a Canaanite woman from that region came and kept crying out, <sup>[13]</sup> “Have mercy on me, Lord, Son of David! My daughter is severely tormented by a demon.” <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.23: Jesus did not say a word to her. His disciples <sup>ⓚ</sup> approached him and urged him, “Send her away because she’s crying out after us.”
CSB17.24: He replied, “I was sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.” <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.25: But she came, knelt before him, and said, “Lord, help me!”
CSB17.26: He answered, “It isn’t right to take the children’s bread and throw it to the dogs.” <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.27: “Yes, Lord,” she said, “yet even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their masters’ table.”
CSB17.28: Then Jesus replied to her, “Woman, your faith is great. Let it be done for you as you want.” And from that moment <sup>[14]</sup> her daughter was healed. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.29: Moving on from there, <sup>ⓐ</sup> Jesus passed along the Sea of Galilee. <sup>ⓑ</sup> He went up on a mountain and sat there,
CSB17.30: and large crowds came to him, including the lame, the blind, the crippled, those unable to speak, and many others. They put them at his feet, and he healed them.
CSB17.31: So the crowd was amazed when they saw those unable to speak talking, the crippled restored, the lame walking, and the blind seeing, <sup>ⓒ</sup> and they gave glory to the God of Israel.
CSB17.32: Jesus called his disciples and said, <sup>ⓓ</sup> “I have compassion on the crowd, because they’ve already stayed with me three days and have nothing to eat. I don’t want to send them away hungry, otherwise they might collapse on the way.”
CSB17.33: The disciples said to him, “Where could we get enough bread in this desolate place <sup>ⓔ</sup> to feed such a crowd?”
CSB17.34: “How many loaves do you have?” Jesus asked them. “Seven,” they said, “and a few small fish.”
CSB17.35: After commanding the crowd to sit down on the ground,
CSB17.36: he took the seven loaves and the fish, gave thanks, broke them, and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.37: They all ate and were satisfied. They collected the leftover pieces — seven large baskets full.
CSB17.38: Now there were four thousand men who had eaten, besides women and children.
CSB17.39: After dismissing the crowds, he got into the boat and went to the region of Magadan. <sup>[15]</sup> <sup>ⓖ</sup>

AMP.1: Then some Pharisees and scribes from Jerusalem came to Jesus and said,
AMP.2: "Why do Your disciples violate the tradition (religious laws) handed down by the [Jewish] elders? For Your disciples do not [ceremonially] wash their hands before they eat."
AMP.3: He replied to them, "Why also do you violate the commandment of God for the sake of your tradition [handed down by the elders]?
AMP.4: For God said [through Moses], 'Honor your father and mother,' and, 'He who speaks evil of or insults or treats improperly father or mother is to be put to death.'
AMP.5: But you say, 'If anyone says to his father or mother, "Whatever [money or resource that] I have that would help you is [already dedicated and] given to God,"
AMP.6: he is not to honor his father or his mother [by helping them with their need].' So by this you have invalidated the word of God [depriving it of force and authority and making it of no effect] for the sake of your tradition [handed down by the elders].
AMP.7: You hypocrites (play-actors, pretenders), rightly did Isaiah prophesy of you when he said,
AMP.8:
'This people honors Me with their lips,
But their heart is far away from Me.
AMP.9:
'But in vain do they worship Me,
For they teach as doctrines the precepts of men.'"
AMP.10: After Jesus called the crowd to Him, He said, "Listen and understand this:
AMP.11: It is not what goes into the mouth of a man that defiles and dishonors him, but what comes out of the mouth, this defiles and dishonors him."
AMP.12: Then the disciples came and said to Jesus, "Do You know that the Pharisees were offended when they heard you say this?"
AMP.13: He answered, "Every plant which My heavenly Father did not plant will be torn up by the roots.
AMP.14: Leave them alone; they are blind guides[leading blind followers]. If a blind man leads a blind man, both will fall into a pit."
AMP.15: Peter asked Him, "Explain this parable [about what defiles a person] to us."
AMP.16: And He said, "Are you still so dull [and unable to put things together]?
AMP.17: Do you not understand that whatever goes into the mouth passes into the stomach, and is eliminated?
AMP.18: But whatever [word] comes out of the mouth comes from the heart, and this is what defiles and dishonors the man.
AMP.19: For out of the heart come evil thoughts and plans, murders, adulteries, sexual immoralities, thefts, false testimonies, slanders (verbal abuse, irreverent speech, blaspheming).
AMP.20: These are the things which defile and dishonor the man; but eating with [ceremonially] unwashed hands does not defile the man."
AMP.21: After leaving there, Jesus withdrew to the district of Tyre and Sidon.
AMP.22: And a Canaanite woman from that district came out and began to cry out [urgently], saying, "Have mercy on me, O Lord, Son of David (Messiah); my daughter is cruelly possessed by a demon."
AMP.23: But He did not say a word in answer to her. And His disciples came and asked Him [repeatedly], "Send her away, because she keeps shouting out after us."
AMP.24: He answered, "I was commissioned by God and sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel."
AMP.25: But she came and began to kneel down before Him, saying, "Lord, help me!"
AMP.26: And He replied, "It is not good (appropriate, fair) to take the children's bread and throw it to the pet dogs."
AMP.27: She said, "Yes, Lord; but even the pet dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their [young] masters' table."
AMP.28: Then Jesus answered her, "Woman, your faith [your personal trust and confidence in My power] is great; it will be done for you as you wish." And her daughter was healed from that moment.
AMP.29: Jesus went on from there and passed along by [the eastern shore of] the Sea of Galilee. Then He went up on the hillside and was sitting there.
AMP.30: And great crowds came to Him, bringing with them the lame, crippled, blind, mute, and many others, and they put them down at His feet; and He healed them.
AMP.31: So the crowd was amazed when they saw the mute speaking, the crippled restored, the lame walking, and the blind seeing; and they praised and glorified the God of Israel.
AMP.32: Then Jesus called His disciples to Him, and said, "I feel compassion for the crowd, because they have been with Me now three days and have nothing [left] to eat; and I do not want to send them away hungry, because they might faint [from exhaustion] on the way [home]."
AMP.33: The disciples said to Him, "Where are we to get enough bread in this isolated place to feed so large a crowd?"
AMP.34: And Jesus asked them, "How many loaves [of bread] do you have?" They replied, "Seven, and a few small fish."
AMP.35: He directed the crowd to sit down on the ground,
AMP.36: and He took the seven loaves and the fish; and when He had given thanks, He broke them and started giving them to the disciples, and the disciples [gave them] to the people.
AMP.37: And they all ate and were satisfied, and they gathered up seven full baskets of the broken pieces that were left over.
AMP.38: [Among] those who ate were 4,000 men, not counting women and children.
AMP.39: Then Jesus sent the crowds away, got into the boat and went to the district of Magadan.

MSG.1: After that, Pharisees and religion scholars came to Jesus all the way from Jerusalem, criticizing,
MSG.2: "Why do your disciples play fast and loose with the rules?"
MSG.3: But Jesus put it right back on them. "Why do you use your rules to play fast and loose with God's commands?
MSG.4: God clearly says, 'Respect your father and mother,' and, 'Anyone denouncing father or mother should be killed.'
MSG.5: But you weasel around that by saying, 'Whoever wants to, can say to father and mother, What I owed to you I've given to God.'
MSG.6: That can hardly be called respecting a parent. You cancel God's command by your rules.
MSG.7: Frauds! Isaiah's prophecy of you hit the bull's-eye:
MSG.8: These people make a big show of saying the right thing, but their heart isn't in it.
MSG.9: They act like they're worshiping me, but they don't mean it. They just use me as a cover for teaching whatever suits their fancy."
MSG.10: He then called the crowd together and said, "Listen, and take this to heart.
MSG.11: It's not what you swallow that pollutes your life, but what you vomit up."
MSG.12: Later his disciples came and told him, "Did you know how upset the Pharisees were when they heard what you said?"
MSG.13: Jesus shrugged it off. "Every tree that wasn't planted by my Father in heaven will be pulled up by its roots.
MSG.14: Forget them. They are blind men leading blind men. When a blind man leads a blind man, they both end up in the ditch."
MSG.15: Peter said, "I don't get it. Put it in plain language."
MSG.16: Jesus replied, "You too? Are you being willfully stupid?
MSG.17: Don't you know that anything that is swallowed works its way through the intestines and is finally defecated?
MSG.18: But what comes out of the mouth gets its start in the heart.
MSG.19: It's from the heart that we vomit up evil arguments, murders, adulteries, fornications, thefts, lies, and cussing.
MSG.20: That's what pollutes. Eating or not eating certain foods, washing or not washing your hands—that's neither here nor there."
MSG.21: From there Jesus took a trip to Tyre and Sidon.
MSG.22: They had hardly arrived when a Canaanite woman came down from the hills and pleaded, "Mercy, Master, Son of David! My daughter is cruelly afflicted by an evil spirit."
MSG.23: Jesus ignored her. The disciples came and complained, "Now she's bothering us. Would you please take care of her? She's driving us crazy."
MSG.24: Jesus refused, telling them, "I've got my hands full dealing with the lost sheep of Israel."
MSG.25: Then the woman came back to Jesus, went to her knees, and begged. "Master, help me."
MSG.26: He said, "It's not right to take bread out of children's mouths and throw it to dogs."
MSG.27: She was quick: "You're right, Master, but beggar dogs do get scraps from the master's table."
MSG.28: Jesus gave in. "Oh, woman, your faith is something else. What you want is what you get!" Right then her daughter became well.
MSG.29: After Jesus returned, he walked along Lake Galilee and then climbed a mountain and took his place, ready to receive visitors.
MSG.30: They came, tons of them, bringing along the paraplegic, the blind, the maimed, the mute—all sorts of people in need—and more or less threw them down at Jesus' feet to see what he would do with them. He healed them.
MSG.31: When the people saw the mutes speaking, the maimed healthy, the paraplegics walking around, the blind looking around, they were astonished and let everyone know that God was blazingly alive among them.
MSG.32: But Jesus wasn't finished with them. He called his disciples and said, "I hurt for these people. For three days now they've been with me, and now they have nothing to eat. I can't send them away without a meal—they'd probably collapse on the road."
MSG.33: His disciples said, "But where in this deserted place are you going to dig up enough food for a meal?"
MSG.34: Jesus asked, "How much bread do you have?" "Seven loaves," they said, "plus a few fish."
MSG.35: At that, Jesus directed the people to sit down.
MSG.36: He took the seven loaves and the fish. After giving thanks, he divided it up and gave it to the people.
MSG.37: Everyone ate. They had all they wanted. It took seven large baskets to collect the leftovers.
MSG.38: Over four thousand people ate their fill at that meal.
MSG.39: After Jesus sent them away, he climbed in the boat and crossed over to the Magadan hills.

NIV.1:
Clean and Unclean
Then some Pharisees and teachers of the law came to Jesus from Jerusalem and asked,
NIV.2: “Why do your disciples break the tradition of the elders? They don’t wash their hands before they eat!”
NIV.3: Jesus replied, “And why do you break the command of God for the sake of your tradition?
NIV.4: For God said, ‘Honor your father and mother’ and ‘Anyone who curses his father or mother must be put to death.’
NIV.5: But you say that if a man says to his father or mother, ‘Whatever help you might otherwise have received from me is a gift devoted to God,’
NIV.6: he is not to ‘honor his father’ with it. Thus you nullify the word of God for the sake of your tradition.
NIV.7: You hypocrites! Isaiah was right when he prophesied about you:
NIV.8:
“‘These people honor me with their lips,
but their hearts are far from me.
NIV.9:
They worship me in vain;
their teachings are but rules taught by men.’”
NIV.10: Jesus called the crowd to him and said, “Listen and understand.
NIV.11: What goes into a man’s mouth does not make him ‘unclean,’ but what comes out of his mouth, that is what makes him ‘unclean.’”
NIV.12: Then the disciples came to him and asked, “Do you know that the Pharisees were offended when they heard this?”
NIV.13: He replied, “Every plant that my heavenly Father has not planted will be pulled up by the roots.
NIV.14: Leave them; they are blind guides. If a blind man leads a blind man, both will fall into a pit.”
NIV.15: Peter said, “Explain the parable to us.”
NIV.16: “Are you still so dull?” Jesus asked them.
NIV.17: “Don’t you see that whatever enters the mouth goes into the stomach and then out of the body?
NIV.18: But the things that come out of the mouth come from the heart, and these make a man ‘unclean.’
NIV.19: For out of the heart come evil thoughts, murder, adultery, sexual immorality, theft, false testimony, slander.
NIV.20: These are what make a man ‘unclean’; but eating with unwashed hands does not make him ‘unclean.’”
NIV.21:
The Faith of the Canaanite Woman
Leaving that place, Jesus withdrew to the region of Tyre and Sidon.
NIV.22: A Canaanite woman from that vicinity came to him, crying out, “Lord, Son of David, have mercy on me! My daughter is suffering terribly from demon-possession.”
NIV.23: Jesus did not answer a word. So his disciples came to him and urged him, “Send her away, for she keeps crying out after us.”
NIV.24: He answered, “I was sent only to the lost sheep of Israel.”
NIV.25: The woman came and knelt before him. “Lord, help me!” she said.
NIV.26: He replied, “It is not right to take the children’s bread and toss it to their dogs.”
NIV.27: “Yes, Lord,” she said, “but even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their masters’ table.”
NIV.28: Then Jesus answered, “Woman, you have great faith! Your request is granted.” And her daughter was healed from that very hour.
NIV.29:
Jesus Feeds the Four Thousand
Jesus left there and went along the Sea of Galilee. Then he went up on a mountainside and sat down.
NIV.30: Great crowds came to him, bringing the lame, the blind, the crippled, the mute and many others, and laid them at his feet; and he healed them.
NIV.31: The people were amazed when they saw the mute speaking, the crippled made well, the lame walking and the blind seeing. And they praised the God of Israel.
NIV.32: Jesus called his disciples to him and said, “I have compassion for these people; they have already been with me three days and have nothing to eat. I do not want to send them away hungry, or they may collapse on the way.”
NIV.33: His disciples answered, “Where could we get enough bread in this remote place to feed such a crowd?”
NIV.34:
“How many loaves do you have?” Jesus asked.
“Seven,” they replied, “and a few small fish.”
NIV.35: He told the crowd to sit down on the ground.
NIV.36: Then he took the seven loaves and the fish, and when he had given thanks, he broke them and gave them to the disciples, and they in turn to the people.
NIV.37: They all ate and were satisfied. Afterward the disciples picked up seven basketfuls of broken pieces that were left over.
NIV.38: The number of those who ate was four thousand, besides women and children.
NIV.39: After Jesus had sent the crowd away, he got into the boat and went to the vicinity of Magadan.

NRSVCE.1: Then Pharisees and scribes came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said,
NRSVCE.2: “Why do your disciples break the tradition of the elders? For they do not wash their hands before they eat.”
NRSVCE.3: He answered them, “And why do you break the commandment of God for the sake of your tradition?
NRSVCE.4: For God said, ‘Honor your father and your mother,’ and, ‘Whoever speaks evil of father or mother must surely die.’
NRSVCE.5: But you say that whoever tells father or mother, ‘Whatever support you might have had from me is given to God,’ then that person need not honor the father.
NRSVCE.6: So, for the sake of your tradition, you make void the word of God.
NRSVCE.7: You hypocrites! Isaiah prophesied rightly about you when he said:
NRSVCE.8:
‘This people honors me with their lips,
but their hearts are far from me;
NRSVCE.9:
in vain do they worship me,
teaching human precepts as doctrines.’”
NRSVCE.10: Then he called the crowd to him and said to them, “Listen and understand:
NRSVCE.11: it is not what goes into the mouth that defiles a person, but it is what comes out of the mouth that defiles.”
NRSVCE.12: Then the disciples approached and said to him, “Do you know that the Pharisees took offense when they heard what you said?”
NRSVCE.13: He answered, “Every plant that my heavenly Father has not planted will be uprooted.
NRSVCE.14: Let them alone; they are blind guides of the blind. And if one blind person guides another, both will fall into a pit.”
NRSVCE.15: But Peter said to him, “Explain this parable to us.”
NRSVCE.16: Then he said, “Are you also still without understanding?
NRSVCE.17: Do you not see that whatever goes into the mouth enters the stomach, and goes out into the sewer?
NRSVCE.18: But what comes out of the mouth proceeds from the heart, and this is what defiles.
NRSVCE.19: For out of the heart come evil intentions, murder, adultery, fornication, theft, false witness, slander.
NRSVCE.20: These are what defile a person, but to eat with unwashed hands does not defile.”
NRSVCE.21: Jesus left that place and went away to the district of Tyre and Sidon.
NRSVCE.22: Just then a Canaanite woman from that region came out and started shouting, “Have mercy on me, Lord, Son of David; my daughter is tormented by a demon.”
NRSVCE.23: But he did not answer her at all. And his disciples came and urged him, saying, “Send her away, for she keeps shouting after us.”
NRSVCE.24: He answered, “I was sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”
NRSVCE.25: But she came and knelt before him, saying, “Lord, help me.”
NRSVCE.26: He answered, “It is not fair to take the children’s food and throw it to the dogs.”
NRSVCE.27: She said, “Yes, Lord, yet even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their masters’ table.”
NRSVCE.28: Then Jesus answered her, “Woman, great is your faith! Let it be done for you as you wish.” And her daughter was healed instantly.
NRSVCE.29: After Jesus had left that place, he passed along the Sea of Galilee, and he went up the mountain, where he sat down.
NRSVCE.30: Great crowds came to him, bringing with them the lame, the maimed, the blind, the mute, and many others. They put them at his feet, and he cured them,
NRSVCE.31: so that the crowd was amazed when they saw the mute speaking, the maimed whole, the lame walking, and the blind seeing. And they praised the God of Israel.
NRSVCE.32: Then Jesus called his disciples to him and said, “I have compassion for the crowd, because they have been with me now for three days and have nothing to eat; and I do not want to send them away hungry, for they might faint on the way.”
NRSVCE.33: The disciples said to him, “Where are we to get enough bread in the desert to feed so great a crowd?”
NRSVCE.34: Jesus asked them, “How many loaves have you?” They said, “Seven, and a few small fish.”
NRSVCE.35: Then ordering the crowd to sit down on the ground,
NRSVCE.36: he took the seven loaves and the fish; and after giving thanks he broke them and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
NRSVCE.37: And all of them ate and were filled; and they took up the broken pieces left over, seven baskets full.
NRSVCE.38: Those who had eaten were four thousand men, besides women and children.
NRSVCE.39: After sending away the crowds, he got into the boat and went to the region of Magadan.

NET.1: Then Pharisees and experts in the law came from Jerusalem to Jesus and said,
NET.2: “Why do your disciples disobey the tradition of the elders? For they don’t wash their hands when they eat.”
NET.3: He answered them, “And why do you disobey the commandment of God because of your tradition?
NET.4: For God said, ‘Honor your father and mother’ and ‘Whoever insults his father or mother must be put to death.’
NET.5: But you say, ‘If someone tells his father or mother, “Whatever help you would have received from me is given to God,”
NET.6: he does not need to honor his father.’ You have nullified the word of God on account of your tradition.
NET.7: Hypocrites! Isaiah prophesied correctly about you when he said,
NET.8: ‘This people honors me with their lips, but their heart is far from me,
NET.9: and they worship me in vain, teaching as doctrines the commandments of men.’”
NET.10: Then he called the crowd to him and said, “Listen and understand.
NET.11: What defiles a person is not what goes into the mouth; it is what comes out of the mouth that defiles a person.”
NET.12: Then the disciples came to him and said, “Do you know that when the Pharisees heard this saying they were offended?”
NET.13: And he replied, “Every plant that my heavenly Father did not plant will be uprooted.
NET.14: Leave them! They are blind guides. If someone who is blind leads another who is blind, both will fall into a pit.”
NET.15: But Peter said to him, “Explain this parable to us.”
NET.16: Jesus said, “Even after all this, are you still so foolish?
NET.17: Don’t you understand that whatever goes into the mouth enters the stomach and then passes out into the sewer?
NET.18: But the things that come out of the mouth come from the heart, and these things defile a person.
NET.19: For out of the heart come evil ideas, murder, adultery, sexual immorality, theft, false testimony, slander.
NET.20: These are the things that defile a person; it is not eating with unwashed hands that defiles a person.”
NET.21: After going out from there, Jesus went to the region of Tyre and Sidon.
NET.22: A Canaanite woman from that area came and cried out, “Have mercy on me, Lord, Son of David! My daughter is horribly demon-possessed!”
NET.23: But he did not answer her a word. Then his disciples came and begged him, “Send her away, because she keeps on crying out after us.”
NET.24: So he answered, “I was sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”
NET.25: But she came and bowed down before him and said, “Lord, help me!”
NET.26: “It is not right to take the children’s bread and throw it to the dogs,” he said.
NET.27: “Yes, Lord,” she replied, “but even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their masters’ table.”
NET.28: Then Jesus answered her, “Woman, your faith is great! Let what you want be done for you.” And her daughter was healed from that hour.
NET.29: When he left there, Jesus went along the Sea of Galilee. Then he went up a mountain, where he sat down.
NET.30: Then large crowds came to him bringing with them the lame, blind, crippled, mute, and many others. They laid them at his feet, and he healed them.
NET.31: As a result, the crowd was amazed when they saw the mute speaking, the crippled healthy, the lame walking, and the blind seeing, and they praised the God of Israel.
NET.32: Then Jesus called the disciples and said, “I have compassion on the crowd, because they have already been here with me three days and they have nothing to eat. I don’t want to send them away hungry since they may faint on the way.”
NET.33: The disciples said to him, “Where can we get enough bread in this desolate place to satisfy so great a crowd?”
NET.34: Jesus said to them, “How many loaves do you have?” They replied, “Seven — and a few small fish.”
NET.35: After instructing the crowd to sit down on the ground,
NET.36: he took the seven loaves and the fish, and after giving thanks, he broke them and began giving them to the disciples, who then gave them to the crowds.
NET.37: They all ate and were satisfied, and they picked up the broken pieces left over, seven baskets full.
NET.38: Not counting children and women, there were four thousand men who ate.
NET.39: After sending away the crowd, he got into the boat and went to the region of Magadan.
